page: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the top 25 percent. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing also demonstrate strong performance. Multi-core operations, database operations, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. However, memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached read blocks is weak, falling into the bottom 25 percent, while small-block cached reads and large-block writes perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7a.xlarge provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of excess compute cores. The inclusion of DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores supports efficient processing for its class. This instance is suited for workloads such as database operations, in-memory caching, text processing, and light web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU and text processing performance, though large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is relatively weak. Overall multi-core, database, and Redis operations perform at average levels. This hardware profile provides qualitative resource density for memory-intensive applications, making the r7a.xlarge well-suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 AMD EPYC cores, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, making it suitable for database and memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 AMD EPYC cores, 32.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 RAM for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 4 dedicated cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and text processing
